ops.title: Harness bug: context:fork skill executions inherit the calling subagent's StructuredOutput tool; a fork ending its turn on it returns a bare 'Skill execution completed' stub and the answer is lost — applies to ANY forked skill invoked from a schema-bound

ops.body:
Claude Code's forked skill execution (`context: fork` frontmatter) does not isolate the caller's tool inventory: a workflow `agent(..., {schema})` wrapper's `StructuredOutput` tool is visible inside the fork, and that tool sets `toolEndsTurn: true`. A fork that "returns" via it ends with no trailing assistant text; the Skill relay harvests only trailing text, so the caller receives the literal 78-char stub `Skill "codex" completed (forked execution). Result: Skill execution completed` and the fork's real answer is discarded.

Diagnosed 2026-07-08 from cc-context session `b09066d0` (workflow `wf_e60273d5-c47`): 18/18 forks spawned fine and ran 1–6 minutes; all 15 stubs = fork ended on the leaked tool; all 3 successes = fork ended with plain text (2 via ReportFindings, which doesn't end the turn). Not load, rate limits, or auth. Applies to ANY `context: fork` skill invoked from a schema-bound subagent, not just codex. Symptom to recognize: a minutes-long Skill call returning the bare stub.

Fix pattern: don't nest Skill forks inside subagents — ship the recipe as a plugin agent (codex plugin 0.9.0 `codex:codex-wrapper`, see [[model-routing-convention]]). Filed upstream 2026-07-08: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/75559.

RESOLVED for codex 2026-07-08: codex plugin **0.10.0** drops `context: fork` entirely — the skill runs inline, so there is no fork and no relay, and `Skill(codex)` works identically from the main loop, subagents, and workflow stages (verified 6/6 PONG, 0 stubs vs 15/18 baseline). The "never Skill(codex) from subagents" rule is retired: both lanes are valid, with `codex:codex-wrapper` canonical for workflow `agentType` routing and big context gathers. The harness bug itself still exists for OTHER `context: fork` skills invoked from schema-bound agents until #75559 lands — this memory stays as the diagnostic for that class.
